title: Dryopteris varia
taxonomy: Dryopteris varia is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris and is native to North America. The family Dryopteridaceae is a part of the larger division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. Dryopteris varia is classified as a vascular plant, meaning it has a vascular system for transporting water, nutrients, and sugars throughout its body. This species is also known as the variable wood fern due to its variable leaf morphology.

intro: Dryopteris varia, also known as the autumn fern or autumn wood fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of D. varia include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, D. varia is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried extract to take advantage of its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects.
